actionable_feedback: Add the two missing supporting exports exactly as named in the rubric: summary_decision_table.csv and calculation_workup.pdf. Place them in the attempt output directory alongside the xlsx artifact.

Either rename or add workbook sheets so that the workbook contains the rubric-required sheet names: inputs, calculations, scenarios, validation, caveats, decision_table. If you prefer different internal sheet names, include a clear, visible mapping sheet (e.g., 'sheet_mapping') that documents how your sheets correspond to the rubric names.

Include an explicit ROL (rate-on-line) definition in the workbook (show numerator and denominator cells) and add a short reconciliation table (or a dedicated 'calculation_workup' sheet/PDF) that recomputes technical_price and implied ROL for each risk_load option used in the sensitivity (low/base/high). Show the exact formulas and intermediate values so reviewers can re-run the arithmetic readily.

Preserve live formulas in the workbook (don't only rely on patched cached values). Ensure key result cells contain formulas referencing the inputs sheet and set workbook calc properties so hosts will recalc. If you must include cached values for presentation reasons, retain the underlying formulas and annotate why caches were inserted.

Add a manifest or generation log file (manifest.json or generation_log.txt) that lists all produced files, the creator script name and version (e.g., build_reinsurance_sensitivity.py), and a mapping of sheet names to rubric names. Mention any non-standard choices (e.g., reinstatement premium handled as a separate note) so graders don't infer missing items.

Re-run the same render/export verification used in the trace (headless LibreOffice -> PDF render) to produce calculation_workup.pdf from the workbook (or assemble the PDF from a human-readable 'calculation_workup' sheet) so the verifier can preview content even if openpyxl is unavailable.

Update the attempt's final_message/stdout to call out the presence and locations of the added exports and the sheet-name mapping to make automatic artifact-contract prechecks and human graders' reviews quick.
artifact_specific_comments: The primary xlsx artifact is present and cached numeric outputs match independent recomputation within tolerance, but the artifact lacks the explicit supporting CSV and PDF exports the rubric demanded.
Workbook XML was patched to include cached numeric results so values display without recalculation; retain formulas and prefer workbook-level calc settings when possible.
failed_or_weak_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'missing_supporting_exports_and_sheet_name_mismatch', 'explanation': 'Required auxiliary exports (summary_decision_table.csv, calculation_workup.pdf) were not found and workbook sheet names (Summary, Assumptions, Sensitivity, Checks, Sources) do not exactly match rubric-required names (inputs, calculations, scenarios, validation, caveats, decision_table).'}
failed_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'missing_supporting_exports_and_sheet_name_mismatch', 'explanation': 'Required auxiliary exports (summary_decision_table.csv, calculation_workup.pdf) were not found and workbook sheet names (Summary, Assumptions, Sensitivity, Checks, Sources) do not exactly match rubric-required names (inputs, calculations, scenarios, validation, caveats, decision_table).'}
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverable: primary workbook produced and numeric outputs are semantically correct within tolerance. Deductions were applied because two rubric-required supporting exports (summary_decision_table.csv and calculation_workup.pdf) are missing and the workbook's sheet names do not match the rubric's exact required names. The workbook also contains patched cached values to display results; please ensure formulas and recalculation properties are clearly present and provide a reconciliation table that defines ROL (numerator/denominator) and demonstrates recomputation for each risk_load scenario.

notes: Numeric differences are ~0.015% and within rubric tolerance (<=0.1%).
openpyxl was unavailable in grader/verifier preview environments; grader used cached values and trace metadata for checks.

recommended_acceptance_criteria_for_revi: Both summary_decision_table.csv and calculation_workup.pdf exist in the attempt output directory and are referenced in final_message.txt/stdout.
Workbook contains rubric-required sheet names or a documented 1:1 mapping sheet; reconciliation table present and matches cached/displayed results within <=0.1% tolerance.
Key result cells preserve formulas and workbook calc mode is set so host recalc will reproduce values (or a note explains why caches are used).
